author: Alex_Dutton
body_html:
<div class="md"><p>dnssec failures at the tld level are brutal, no amount of your own redundancy saves you when the chain of trust breaks upstream</p>
</div>

author: mrtrly
body_html:
<div class="md"><p>DNSSEC fails closed by design which is the whole point, but it makes these incidents binary in a way most infra failures aren&#39;t. hit a smaller version on a customer zone last year. stale DS record after a key rollover took everything dark for about 4 hours and the only fix was waiting for the registrar to publish the correction. users on non-validating resolvers never noticed, users on 1.1.1.1 or quad9 were just gone. did the writeup mention how long bad records sat in resolver caches after denic pushed the fix?</p>
</div>
